tex: Write the ratio 60 grams : 3 kilograms in the form $1:n$.
Write down the order of rotational symmetry of a regular decagon.

Pedro makes cards.

(a) He makes cards at a rate of 9 cards every 20 minutes.

Work out the number of cards he makes in 8 hours.

(b) Each card costs 12 cents to make.

Pedro sells each card for 50 cents.

Work out his percentage profit on each card.

Nuwa is buying a phone.

One website sells the phone for 953 Yuan.

A different website sells the same phone for \$141.

The exchange rate is 1 Yuan = \$0.152.

Calculate the difference between these phone prices.

Give your answer in dollars, correct to the nearest cent.

One morning, a dentist has appointments for 10 patients.

The stem-and-leaf diagram shows the waiting time for 8 of these patients.

\begin{InsertScreenShot}
\begin{tabular}{c|l}
0 & 1 \quad 4 \\
1 & 0 \quad 2 \quad 9 \\
2 & 1 \quad 5 \quad 5
\end{tabular}

Key: 1 $|$ 0 represents 10 minutes
\end{InsertScreenShot}

The times for the two other patients, $P$ and $Q$, are not shown in the stem-and-leaf diagram.

The mean waiting time of all 10 patients that morning is 16 minutes.

The range of waiting times is 26 minutes.

Patient $P$ waits longer than patient $Q$.

Find the waiting time for each of patient $P$ and patient $Q$.
texHtml:

The range of waiting times is 26 minutes. Patient $P$ waits longer than patient $Q$. Find the waiting time for each of patient $P$ and patient $Q$.

tex:
The diagram shows a parallelogram.

The parallelogram has the same perimeter as a circle with radius 4~m.

(a) Show that $x = 6.06$~m, correct to 2 decimal places.

(b) 

The floor of a room is in the shape of this parallelogram.

It costs \$18 per square metre to tile the floor.

Calculate the total cost of tiling the floor.

(a) On the diagram, draw the image of

(i) (b) shape $A$ after a translation by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} 1 \\ -7 \end{pmatrix}$

(c) shape $A$ after a reflection in the line $y = x + 1$.

(d) Describe fully the **single** transformation that maps shape $A$ onto shape $B$.

...

The diagram shows the speed–time graph for part of a car journey.

Find the total distance travelled in the 25 minutes.

Find the $n$th term of each sequence.

(a) $17, \quad 9, \quad 1, \quad -7, \quad -15, \quad \ldots$

(b) $3, \quad 12, \quad 27, \quad 48, \quad 75, \quad \ldots$

tex:
$A$, $B$ and $C$ lie on a circle centre $O$.

$DE$ is a tangent to the circle at $C$.

Angle $ABC = 52°$ and angle $BCE = 65°$.

(a) Find the value of $x$.

Give a geometrical reason for your answer.

 because ...

(b) Find the value of $y$.

Simplify.

$$\frac{7}{2m} + \frac{3}{8m}$$

(a) Carlos invests \$24000 at a rate of 3.2\% per year compound interest.

Calculate the value of his investment at the end of 4 years.

(b) Carlos buys a painting for \$$x$.

He sells the painting for \$40870.

He makes a profit of 34\%.

Calculate the value of his profit.

(c) Carlos also buys a car with a value of \$32500.

The value of the car decreases exponentially by 23\% each year.

Find a formula for the value, \$$V$, of the car at the end of $n$ years.

The diagram shows a field, $ABCD$, in the shape of a quadrilateral.

$BD$ is a straight path across the field.

(a) Calculate $BC$.

(b) Calculate angle $DBC$.

(c) The total area of the field, $ABCD$, is 35\,900~m$^2$.

Work out the length of the shortest distance from $D$ to $AB$.

The histogram shows information about the masses of some coconuts.

The masses are classified into four categories $A$, $B$, $C$ and $D$.

(a) Show that there are 10 coconuts in category $D$.

(b) Two of the coconuts from those in category $C$ and category $D$ are chosen at random.

Find the probability that both are from category $D$.

(c) Calculate an estimate of the mean mass of the coconuts.

Expand and simplify.

$$(x - 2)(2x + 3)(x + 4)$$

In the diagram, $OA$ is parallel to $CB$.

$OA : CB = 4 : 3$

$\overrightarrow{OA} = \mathbf{a}$ and $\overrightarrow{OB} = \mathbf{b}$.

(a) Find $\overrightarrow{AB}$ in terms of $\mathbf{a}$ and $\mathbf{b}$.

(b) $M$ is the midpoint of $OC$.

Find $\overrightarrow{AM}$ in terms of $\mathbf{a}$ and $\mathbf{b}$.

Give your answer in its simplest form.

Solve the simultaneous equations.

You must show all your working and give your answers correct to 2 decimal places.

\begin{align*}
y &= 5 - 2x \\
y &= 3x^2 - 7x - 6
\end{align*}

A solid metal prism has a mass of 4810~g, correct to the nearest 10~g.

The density of the metal is 7.7~g/cm$^3$, correct to 1 decimal place.

Calculate the lower bound for the volume of the prism.

{[Density = mass $\div$ volume]}

$y$ is inversely proportional to $(x + 2)^2$.

$w$ is proportional to $x$.

When $y = 8$, $x = 3.5$.

When $w = 15$, $x = 90$.

Find $y$ in terms of $w$.
